results.raw_output:
You are viewing the File Explorer window in Windows. At the top right of the screen, there is a small button with an "X" inside a square (the close button). This button is typically used to close the current window. 

The rest of the interface shows options for opening, editing, or sharing files and folders, but none of these will close the File Explorer window itself.

To close this window, simply click on the close button at the top-right corner of the File Explorer window.

To minimize the current window, you need to interact with its controls. At the top right corner of the window, there is a set of controls including an "X" button (often used as a shortcut for minimizing). Clicking this "X" button will minimize the window while still allowing access from the taskbar or dock if needed. This aligns with the goal of minimizing the current window.

The goal is to play the "Microsoft Solitaire Collection" game, as shown in the image. To do this, I need to click on the prominent card image of the Solitaire Collection at the top left corner of the screen. This will start the game and allow me to begin playing. The interface clearly displays multiple solitaire games under the Microsoft Casual Games brand, so selecting the correct one is essential. Therefore, my next action is to click on the "Microsoft Solitaire Collection" tile.

To open the gallery, you need to click on the "Photos" icon in the lower right corner of the screen. This is a common location for the Photos app shortcut within the Windows Start menu. Once clicked, you will be able to access the photo gallery.

To adjust the style of the PowerPoint icon, you need to access the formatting options. In the image, I see a dropdown menu labeled "Style" at the top right corner of the shape containing the text "PowerPoint." Clicking on this dropdown will allow you to choose from various styling options such as fill color, outline, effects, etc., which is typically what users want when they say "adjust the style." Therefore, my next step would be to click on this "Style" dropdown to open up those customization choices.
